entity.name: tonyantony300
entity.description: Solo maintainer of AltSendme, an open-source, cross-platform desktop app for frictionless peer-to-peer file transfer. The work focuses on practical privacy (QUIC + TLS 1.3), no accounts, and performance for large files and folders. There is no prior grant or sponsorship history; funding would go directly toward sustainable development and hardware.

projects.name: AltSendme
projects.description: AltSendme is a minimal cross-platform desktop application for sending files and directories peer-to-peer anywhere in the world. It is built around the Iroh networking stack and uses open public connection relays for NAT traversal and hole punching. Transfers use strong end-to-end encryption (TLS 1.3 over QUIC), with support for large files, folders, pause and resume, and verified streaming.

channels.description: Support AltSendme via bank transfer (monthly or one-time).
Support AltSendme via GitHub Sponsors (monthly or one-time).
One-off tips through Buy Me a Coffee.

plans.name: Developer laptop
plans.description: One-time goal for a reliable laptop to build, test, and release AltSendme on Windows and Linux. Currently relying on friends and contributors for windows and linux features & testing.
